narrative_md: Secondary pattern: some implementations (notably fhir-to-omop-demo) create a care_site row from each FHIR Location resource, in addition to the primary care_site row from Organization. The Location-derived care_site row carries the location_id (linking to the location row from the same Location resource) while the Organization-derived row carries the name and place_of_service. The two rows share the same care_site_id and must be merged downstream. This pattern is NOT used by omoponfhir-v54, ETL-German, or NACHC.

fields.notes: Resolves to the Organization's care_site_id. Same care_site_id as the row produced by Organization -> care_site. The two rows describe the same care site.

sources.comment: Derives care_site_id from managingOrganization reference; same ID as Organization-derived row — only fhir-to-omop-demo uses this dual-row pattern

fields.notes: Typically null on this row. Set by the Organization-derived care_site row, not this one.
Typically null on this row. Set by the Organization-derived care_site row, not this one.
Points to the location row produced from this same Location resource (see Location__location edge).

sources.comment: Sets location_id = Location.id on the second care_site row, linking back to the location row from the same resource — unique to fhir-to-omop-demo dual-row pattern

fields.notes: Typically null on this row. Set by the Organization-derived care_site row.
Typically null on this row. Set by the Organization-derived care_site row.

references.notes: Second care_site row from Location resource: lines 31-39. care_site_id from managingOrganization (line 10, 33), location_id = .id (line 36).
Organization-derived care_site row (no location_id): lines 11-22. location_id null in this row (line 17).

edge_cases.handling: No care_site_id available -- skip the care_site row. The location row is still emitted via the Location__location edge.

edge_cases.case: Location.managingOrganization references Organization not in bundle
edge_cases.handling: Deferred resolution. Stub care_site_id; resolve when Organization arrives.

edge_cases.case: Multiple Locations under the same managingOrganization
edge_cases.handling: Each Location emits a care_site row with the same care_site_id. Without merge, this produces duplicates -- a single Organization with N Locations yields 1 + N care_site rows.

edge_cases.case: Location with no address but with managingOrganization
edge_cases.handling: care_site row emitted but location_id points at a sparse location row (lat/lng only or empty).

edge_cases.case: Location with address but no managingOrganization
edge_cases.handling: location row only -- no care_site row from this edge.

edge_cases.case: Duplicate care_site rows from Organization and Location
edge_cases.handling: Merge before insert: group rows by care_site_id and coalesce non-null fields (name from Organization row, location_id from Location row). Or pick Organization row authoritatively and lose location_id linkage.
